title: Module init leaks module reference on `PyType_Ready` failure and ignores `PyModule_AddObject` errors
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

The C extension's module initialisation in `src/wrapt/_wrappers.c` (`moduleinit()`) has two related cleanup bugs on cold error paths.

## Problem 1 — Module reference leaked on `PyType_Ready` failure

After `PyModule_Create(&moduledef)` succeeds, six successive `PyType_Ready()` calls each `return NULL` on failure without `Py_DECREF`-ing the freshly created module object. Any failure here strands the module reference owned by the init function.

## Problem 2 — Unchecked `PyModule_AddObject()` calls

The six `PyModule_AddObject()` calls that publish the proxy/wrapper types onto the module are not checked for failure. `PyModule_AddObject()` only steals its reference on success, so:

1. **Return value ignored.** A failure is silently dropped on the floor.
2. **Type reference leaked.** Each call site does a `Py_INCREF` immediately before `PyModule_AddObject()`. On failure the steal does not happen, but the corresponding `Py_DECREF` is never issued, permanently inflating the (statically allocated) type's refcount.
3. **Pending exception is not honoured.** If one of the calls fails it sets an exception, but the function then continues to issue further `PyModule_AddObject()` calls with a pending exception, and finally returns the module object as if everything succeeded. Returning a non-`NULL` module with an exception set violates the C-API contract for module init and surfaces as a confusing `SystemError: ... returned a result with an exception set` from the import machinery instead of a clean `ImportError`.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
